Caption: Illustration of the concept of this study. DiI dye was embedded in the lipid shell of MBs to mimic a small-molecule lipophilic drug. The size of the AVT-trapped DiI-MBs clusters was evaluated to estimate their resonance frequency. Next, these clusters excited by on-resonance US at 100 kHz were monitored using high-speed microscopy imaging and a PCD transducer to analyze the oscillation dynamics and acoustic disruption threshold, respectively. Additionally, the release of fluorescent DiI dye from the MBs clusters was monitored and measured to quantify their payload release.
